project.name: Digital Spotter
project.description: Edge-based multimodal AI posture correction system for compound barbell lifts. Real-time BlazePose vision + IMU sensor fusion with TFLite LSTM inference on Raspberry Pi 4B.
project.author: bimalawijekoon

agent_instructions.role: You are a senior full-stack embedded AI engineer. Your job is to build the Digital Spotter production system exactly as specified in this plan. Follow each phase sequentially. Never skip a phase. After completing each file, run the corresponding test. Only proceed to the next phase when the current phase test passes. Always write production-grade, commented, scalable code.
coding_rules: Every file must have a header comment block with: filename, purpose, author, version, last_modified
Every function must have a docstring explaining inputs, outputs, and side effects
Every API endpoint must have error handling with proper HTTP status codes
Every placeholder must be clearly marked with: # TODO: PHASE_X - description
All magic numbers must be constants defined at the top of the file
All configuration must come from config.py or environment variables, never hardcoded
All file paths must use pathlib.Path, never string concatenation
Python files must pass flake8 linting with max line length 100
JavaScript must use const/let, never var
CSS must use CSS variables for all colors, sizes, and fonts
file_creation_rules: Create files exactly at the paths specified in project_structure
Never create files outside the specified structure
Always create __init__.py in every Python package directory
Always create a README.md in every major directory
Run the test immediately after creating each file or group of files
communication_rules: After each phase, print a PHASE COMPLETE summary with: files created, tests passed, next phase
If a test fails, fix it before moving on — never skip
If a placeholder is added, log it to PLACEHOLDERS.md
Keep a CHANGELOG.md updated after each phase

frontend.core: Vanilla HTML5 + CSS3 + ES2022 JavaScript — no framework, zero dependencies, runs directly from Pi
frontend.video: WebRTC WHEP protocol via mediamtx — hardware H264 GPU encode, sub-150ms latency
frontend.realtime: Native WebSocket API — live pose data, angles, inference results
frontend.charts: Chart.js 4.x CDN — joint angle time series, session history graphs
frontend.styling: CSS Grid + Flexbox + CSS Variables — dark theme, responsive, no preprocessor needed
frontend.fonts: Space Grotesk + Space Mono via Google Fonts CDN

backend.framework: Flask 3.x + Flask-SocketIO 5.x — lightweight, runs well on Pi 4B
backend.websocket: python-socketio with eventlet — async WebSocket for real-time data push
backend.pose: MediaPipe 0.10.x Tasks API — BlazePose Heavy model, float16
backend.inference: tflite-runtime — quantized LSTM model, sub-25ms per sequence
backend.imu: paho-mqtt — MQTT subscriber for ESP32-S3 IMU data (future phase)
backend.database: SQLite3 via Python stdlib — local session storage, zero config
backend.camera_stream: mediamtx v1.9.1 — WebRTC + RTSP server with rpiCamera source

devops.process_manager: systemd — autostart all services on boot
devops.reverse_proxy: nginx — single port 80 entry point (future phase)
devops.remote_dev: VS Code Remote SSH — edit files directly on Pi
devops.version_control: git — local repo on Pi, push to GitHub
devops.testing: pytest + unittest — isolated unit tests per module

config/.purpose: All configuration constants and environment settings

api/.purpose: Flask REST API and WebSocket server

inference/.purpose: TFLite LSTM model inference and feature engineering

database/.purpose: SQLite session storage and query helpers

scripts/.purpose: Dev and ops scripts for Pi management

phases.name: Project Bootstrap
phases.description: Create full project skeleton, install dependencies, initialize git, verify environment

commands: git add . && git commit -m 'chore: initial project bootstrap'

test.command: python3 -c "import flask, mediapipe, numpy, cv2, joblib; print('PHASE_0 PASS: All deps OK')"

phases.name: Configuration Layer
phases.description: Create the central config system. All other modules import from here. Zero hardcoded values anywhere else.

files_to_create.purpose: Master configuration — all settings in one place
Immutable constants — joint names, angle indices, error categories

test.command: cd ~/digital-spotter && python3 -c "from config.config import Config; from config.constants import JOINT_NAMES; print('PHASE_1 PASS:', Config.API.PORT, JOINT_NAMES[0])"

phases.name: Database Layer
phases.description: SQLite schema, models, and query helpers. Sessions, reps, angles, inference results all stored here.

files_to_create.purpose: SQLite schema — all tables defined here
Database connection manager — thread-safe SQLite access
Data classes for Session, Rep, SystemLog
All SQL queries as named functions — no raw SQL anywhere else

phases.name: Vision Pipeline
phases.description: MediaPipe BlazePose pose estimation, joint angle calculation, and rep segmentation. This is the core of the vision station.

files_to_create.purpose: MediaPipe BlazePose initialization and landmark extraction
methods: process_frame(self, frame: np.ndarray) -> PoseResult — runs inference on single frame
get_landmarks(self, pose_result) -> dict — extracts 33 landmarks as {name: (x,y,z,visibility)}
is_valid_frame(self, landmarks) -> bool — checks visibility thresholds
draw_skeleton(self, frame, landmarks) -> np.ndarray — draws skeleton overlay on frame
files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_3 - Add BlazePose Heavy float16 weights path when model file is deployed

files_to_create.purpose: Compute 7 clinical joint angles from 3D landmark coordinates
methods: calculate_angle(a, b, c) -> float — arctangent dot-product formula for 3-point joint vectors
compute_all_angles(landmarks) -> dict[str, float] — returns all 7 angles

files_to_create.purpose: Detect repetitions and segment into 3 temporal phases using Savitzky-Golay smoothing
methods: __init__(self, fps=30) — initializes angle buffer and smoothing params
update(self, angles: dict) -> RepEvent|None — add frame angles, returns RepEvent if rep detected
reset(self) — clear buffer for new session

files_to_create.purpose: Orchestrates the full frame processing pipeline: capture -> pose -> angles -> segmentation
methods: process(self, frame) -> FrameResult — full pipeline for single frame
start(self) — begin processing loop in background thread
get_latest(self) -> FrameResult — thread-safe latest result getter
files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_7 - Replace frame source with direct libcamera capture instead of video file for production

test_cases: test_angle_calculator_known_values — use hardcoded 90-degree vectors, assert angle == 90
test_rep_segmenter_detects_rep — feed synthetic sine wave angles, assert 1 rep detected
test_rep_segmenter_assigns_phases — assert 3 phases returned for 1 rep

phases.name: Inference Pipeline
phases.description: TFLite LSTM runner, feature engineering matching training pipeline exactly, sequence buffer for 3-phase windowing.
phases.critical_note: Feature engineering MUST exactly match the preprocessing pipeline from the paper. Same 38 features, same StandardScaler, same column order. Any mismatch = wrong predictions.

files_to_create.purpose: Load and apply the joblib StandardScaler from training
methods: transform(self, features: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray — applies same scaling as training
files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_4 - Replace placeholder scaler with actual scaler.pkl exported from training notebook

files_to_create.purpose: Transforms raw angles + IMU data into the exact 38 features used in training

feature_groups.computed_angles: 7 features — bilateral mean + L-R asymmetry for hip/knee/ankle + trunk
feature_groups.imu_raw: 6 features — IMU_Acc_X/Y/Z + IMU_Gyro_X/Y/Z (placeholder zeros until PHASE_6)
feature_groups.context: 6 features — Rep_Phase, Exercise_ID, Load_kg, Subject_Height, Subject_Weight, FTR

methods: engineer(self, landmarks, angles, imu_data, context) -> np.ndarray — returns (38,) feature vector

files_to_create.purpose: Buffer to accumulate 3 phase feature vectors before running LSTM inference
methods: push(self, phase_id, features) — add phase features
is_ready(self) -> bool — True when all 3 phases collected

files_to_create.purpose: Load TFLite model and run inference on (3, 38) sequence
methods: load(self, model_path, scaler_path) — loads .tflite and scaler.pkl
predict(self, sequence: np.ndarray) -> InferenceResult — runs model, returns label + confidence
_preprocess(self, sequence) -> np.ndarray — scale and reshape to (1, 3, 38)

InferenceResult.label: str — Good Form or Bad Form

files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_4 - Replace placeholder model with actual model_quantized.tflite from training. Until then, return mock InferenceResult with random confidence.

phases.name: Flask API + WebSocket Server
phases.description: REST API for session management and control. WebSocket for real-time live data streaming to frontend.

files_to_create.purpose: Flask app factory — creates and configures the Flask application
All REST API endpoints as Flask Blueprint

endpoints.purpose: Get session details and rep history
Test inference with dummy data — for development

files_to_create.purpose: WebSocket event handlers and emitters using Flask-SocketIO
events_emitted: pose_data — {landmarks, angles, fps, timestamp_ms} every frame
inference_result — {label, confidence, is_bad_form, rep_number} on rep complete
rep_complete — {rep_number, phase, angles} when rep phase detected
files_to_create.emit_rate: pose_data emitted at max 30fps, throttled to avoid flooding

files_to_create.purpose: Request middleware — CORS, logging, rate limiting
functions: cors_headers(response) — add CORS headers for local network access
log_request() — log every API call with timing

phases.name: Production Frontend Dashboard
phases.description: Full production HTML dashboard. WebRTC video, canvas pose overlay, real-time angle charts, rep counter, form feedback, session management. All wired to WebSocket API.

layout.structure: CSS Grid — 3 column layout on desktop, collapsible on tablet/mobile

sections: Session Control Panel — exercise selector, start/stop buttons, session timer
Rep Counter — large rep number display, good/bad split
Current Phase Indicator — Eccentric/Isometric/Concentric with animation
Form Alert — GOOD FORM / BAD FORM with confidence bar and error category
Video Feed — WebRTC stream in <video> tag, 16:9 aspect ratio
Canvas Overlay — transparent <canvas> over video for skeleton drawing
Feedback Bar — horizontal progress bar showing form confidence
Connection Status Bar — latency, fps, stream quality indicators
Joint Angles Panel — 7 angle readouts with min/max/current and color coding
Live Angle Chart — Chart.js line chart, last 60 frames, knee flexion highlighted
Session History — last 5 sessions with quick stats
System Panel — Pi health, camera status, model status, uptime

files_to_create.purpose: Shell HTML — imports CSS and JS, defines layout grid, no inline styles or scripts
files_to_create.note: Loads css/main.css, css/components.css, js/main.js as modules

files_to_create.purpose: Global styles, CSS variables, layout grid, typography
Individual UI component styles — cards, buttons, badges, charts
CSS animations — pulse live dot, phase transitions, alert flash, skeleton draw
WebRTC connection manager — connects to mediamtx WHEP endpoint
methods: connect(host, port, path) — establish WebRTC peer connection
onStats(callback) — fired every 1.5s with RTCStats data

files_to_create.purpose: Socket.IO client — connects to Flask-SocketIO API
methods: emit(event, data) — send event to server

files_to_create.purpose: Canvas overlay — draws BlazePose skeleton on top of video
methods: init(videoEl, canvasEl) — size canvas to match video
drawAngleAnnotation(joint, angle) — draw angle value next to joint
_scaleToCanvas(x, y) — convert normalized coords to canvas px
files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_3 - landmarks from WebSocket will replace placeholder random data

files_to_create.purpose: Chart.js angle time series chart — rolling 60-frame window
methods: update(angles) — push new frame angles, shift oldest
highlight(jointName) — bold the selected joint line

chart_config.datasets: one line per joint angle (7 total)

files_to_create.purpose: Orchestrates all UI components — connects stream, websocket, overlay, charts
methods: init() — boot sequence: connect stream, connect websocket, init overlay, init chart
onPoseData(data) — update overlay, charts, angle cards
onInferenceResult(result) — update form alert, rep counter
onRepComplete(rep) — increment counter, log to history
startSession(exerciseId) — API call + UI state change
stopSession() — API call + show summary modal

files_to_create.purpose: Entry point — instantiates UIController and boots the app
files_to_create.content: import UIController; const app = new UIController(); app.init();

pose_data.note: UI must render cleanly with this placeholder data before real vision pipeline is connected

inference_result.note: Form alert and confidence bar must render correctly with this placeholder

test_cases: test_webrtc_stream_connects — check mediamtx is running and WHEP responds

phases.name: Integration — Connect All Modules
phases.description: Wire vision pipeline to API. Wire inference to rep events. Wire WebSocket to frontend. Run full pipeline end-to-end with placeholder model.

integration_points.how: FrameProcessor runs in background thread, on each frame result calls socketio.emit('pose_data', result)
On phase complete, RepSegmenter calls SequenceBuffer.push(phase_id, features)
All API endpoints use queries.py functions exclusively

phases.name: IMU Placeholder Layer
phases.description: Stub IMU module so the rest of the system is IMU-aware but not IMU-dependent. When ESP32-S3 is connected, just activate the MQTT subscriber.

files_to_create.purpose: MQTT subscriber for ESP32-S3 IMU data
methods: connect(broker, port, topic) — connect to MQTT broker
on_message(client, userdata, msg) — parse IMU packet
get_latest() -> IMUReading|None — thread-safe latest reading getter
files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_8 - Set config.MQTT.ENABLED = True and configure ESP32-S3 MQTT broker IP when hardware is ready

files_to_create.purpose: Parse and process raw IMU packets from ESP32-S3
methods: parse_packet(raw: bytes) -> IMUReading — parse tab-delimited MQTT payload
compute_derived_features(readings) -> dict — V_bar, P_bar, Smoothness_Jerk per phase

files_to_create.purpose: Merge vision and IMU data at 100ms tolerance — merge-as-of join
methods: push_vision(timestamp_ms, vision_features) — add vision data point
push_imu(timestamp_ms, imu_features) — add IMU data point
files_to_create.placeholder_note: TODO: PHASE_6 - FusionEngine returns vision-only features until IMU is connected. IMU features are zeros.

phases.name: Scripts and DevOps
phases.description: Management scripts for easy Pi control. systemd services. Deploy script from laptop. Health dashboard.

files_to_create.purpose: Start all services: mediamtx + Flask API + frontend server
files_to_create.content_spec: Kill existing processes, start mediamtx in background, start Flask in background, start python http.server, print URLs

files_to_create.purpose: Gracefully stop all services
files_to_create.content_spec: pkill mediamtx, pkill flask, pkill python3 http.server, confirm stopped

files_to_create.purpose: Print status of all services, URLs, camera, model files
files_to_create.content_spec: Check each process, curl /api/health, check file existence, print summary table

files_to_create.purpose: Run from LAPTOP via SSH to push code changes and restart services
files_to_create.content_spec: rsync ~/digital-spotter/ to Pi, SSH in and restart services, tail logs for 5s
files_to_create.usage: Run from LAPTOP: bash deploy.sh 192.168.1.8 bimalawijekoon

files_to_create.purpose: Fresh install of all system dependencies on a new Pi
files_to_create.content_spec: apt installs, pip installs, systemd service setup, directory creation

phases.name: Testing Suite Completion
phases.description: Complete all test files. Run full test suite. Each subsystem independently testable.
phases.test_philosophy: Test each system in isolation first, then integration. Never test two unknowns at once.

test_sequence.purpose: Verify camera hardware, detect camera, check FPS
Verify MediaPipe loads, angles compute correctly, reps detected
Verify model loads, features correct shape, latency under 25ms
Verify all endpoints return correct schemas, WebSocket connects
Verify schema, CRUD operations, session flow
Verify HTML served, JS/CSS accessible, WebRTC connects
Verify full pipeline camera-to-feedback works end to end

future_phases.PHASE_11: Firebase integration — sync sessions to cloud, React dashboard at firebase URL
future_phases.PHASE_12: ESP32-S3 live MQTT integration — full multimodal fusion as per paper
future_phases.PHASE_13: Multiclass LSTM — predict specific error type (Knee Valgus, Spinal Rounding etc)
future_phases.PHASE_14: Audio feedback — text-to-speech on Pi using pyttsx3 or espeak
future_phases.PHASE_15: Mobile PWA — installable Progressive Web App for phone viewing
future_phases.PHASE_16: NGINX reverse proxy — single port 80, proper SSL, domain name on local network

agent_start_command: Begin with PHASE_0. Create the directory structure first. Then proceed phase by phase. Do not skip any phase. Do not mark a phase complete until its test passes. After all phases complete, run the full test suite and confirm all tests pass. Then print the QUICK REFERENCE table so the user can access everything immediately.
